summary: The blocker from iteration 2 is fixed: app/assets/tailwind/application.css line 8 now reads 'raw gray/white shades' with no embedded `*/`, so the CSS comment no longer swallows the light-mode `:root` block. Rebuilt the Tailwind bundle and confirmed app/assets/builds/tailwind.css now contains both the `:root { ... }` light-mode custom-property defaults (--color-app, --color-surface, --color-border, --color-text, etc.) and the `.dark { ... }` overrides, so default (light/system-light) styling is restored. bin/rubocop is clean (145 files, no offenses). Ran the Minitest suite in the test environment (bin/rails test, PARALLEL_WORKERS=0 to avoid a known fork-safety segfault in this Ruby/libpq/pg combination when running parallel workers): 184 runs, 660 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 skips. No development or production database was touched — only bin/rubocop and bin/rails test (RAILS_ENV=test) were run.
